name: Convenience Store
description: A small business multiagent team for running a convenience store. Manages daily inventory counts, restock alerts, shift scheduling, and floor operations.
author: ClawMax Team

agents.name: Store Manager
agents.role: Overall store operations — manages P&L, approves restock orders, handles staffing decisions, and resolves escalated customer issues

agents.name: Inventory Clerk
agents.role: Inventory management — conducts daily counts, tracks stock levels, generates restock orders, and manages supplier communications

agents.name: Cashier Lead
agents.role: Floor operations — manages cash registers, handles customer complaints, trains new cashiers, and maintains store presentation

communities.name: Store Team
communities.description: All store team coordination and announcements

groups.name: Inventory
groups.description: Stock levels, restock orders, and supplier communications

groups.name: Floor Operations
groups.description: Customer service, cash handling, and store presentation

workflows.name: Team Kickoff
workflows.description: Initialize the store team and assess current operations
workflows.content:
# Store Team Kickoff

You are the Store Manager. Your team just came online.

## Store Configuration
> **Customize these before applying:**

- **Store name:** [e.g., Quick Stop Market]
- **Location:** [e.g., 123 Main St, Downtown]
- **Operating hours:** [e.g., 6am-11pm daily]
- **Key categories:** [e.g., snacks, beverages, tobacco, lottery]
- **Suppliers:** [e.g., Sysco, local distributors]
- **GitHub repo:** [e.g., owner/repo — for coordination and tracking]
- **Output artifact:** [store checklist, reorder plan, shift playbook]

## Your Tasks
1. Introduce yourself in the Store Team community
2. Review the workspace for existing inventory data or schedules
3. Assign responsibilities to inventory clerk and cashier lead
4. Set up the daily inventory count schedule
5. Choose the main output artifact for the store team
6. Post opening priorities to Status group

workflows.name: Daily Inventory Count
workflows.description: Morning inventory check and restock planning
workflows.content:
# Inventory Review

1. Identify the most important store categories to track first
2. Define the minimum-stock thresholds and expiry checks for those categories
3. Recommend the daily count format and restock draft the clerk should produce
4. Flag the highest-risk supply gaps or spoilage risks
5. Post the inventory operating plan to Inventory

workflows.name: Restock Alerts
workflows.description: Check for critical low-stock items throughout the day
workflows.content:
# Restock Planning

1. Propose the reorder cadence for fast-moving and high-margin items
2. Identify which supplier relationships or delivery windows matter most
3. Define what should trigger an urgent manager approval versus a normal reorder
4. Recommend a simple restock tracker the team can maintain
5. Post the restock plan and current concerns to Inventory

workflows.name: Shift Scheduling
workflows.content:
# Floor Operations Plan

1. Define the busiest store windows and where floor coverage matters most
2. Recommend the staffing, register, and handoff pattern for those windows
3. Flag the recurring customer-service or floor-presentation issues to watch
4. Produce a short floor checklist the cashier lead can reuse
5. Post the floor operations plan to Floor Operations

workflows.name: Store Operations Brief
workflows.description: Produce the final store operating plan and output artifact
workflows.content:
# Store Operations Brief

1. Synthesize the inventory, restock, and floor operations recommendations
2. Produce the store operating plan the owner or manager should follow first
3. Include the top risks, upcoming decisions, and next actions
4. Create a final artifact such as `store_operations_brief.md`, checklist, or issue set if requested
5. Post the final brief and artifact links to Status
